title: flake: "Podman ps" test has 'podman run -d ...' exit 125 instead of 0
body:
In a recent master run, Travis failed [with][1]

```
• Failure [13.176 seconds]
Podman ps
/go/src/github.com/projectatomic/libpod/test/e2e/ps_test.go:14
  podman --sort by command [It]
  /go/src/github.com/projectatomic/libpod/test/e2e/ps_test.go:227
  Expected
      <int>: 125
  to equal
      <int>: 0
  /go/src/github.com/projectatomic/libpod/test/e2e/ps_test.go:235
  Full Stack Trace
  	/go/src/github.com/onsi/gomega/internal/assertion/assertion.go:69 +0x1ed
  github.com/onsi/gomega/internal/assertion.(*Assertion).To(0xc420cfbbc0, 0xd10660, 0xc4203e0000, 0x0, 0x0, 0x0, 0xc420cfbbc0)
  	/go/src/github.com/onsi/gomega/internal/assertion/assertion.go:35 +0xae
  github.com/projectatomic/libpod/test/e2e.glob..func19.19()
  	/go/src/github.com/projectatomic/libpod/test/e2e/ps_test.go:235 +0x2cd
  github.com/onsi/ginkgo/internal/leafnodes.(*runner).runSync(0xc4203d7380, 0x0, 0x0, 0x0, 0x0, 0x0, 0x0, 0x0, 0x0, 0x0, ...)
  	/go/src/github.com/projectatomic/libpod/test/e2e/libpod_suite_test.go:97 +0x13f
  testing.tRunner(0xc4204ca0f0, 0xca4188)
  	/usr/local/go/src/testing/testing.go:777 +0xd0
  created by testing.(*T).Run
  	/usr/local/go/src/testing/testing.go:824 +0x2e0
```

The failing call [is][2]:

```go
session = podmanTest.Podman([]string{"run", "-d", fedoraMinimal, "pwd"})
```

This may be another symptom of #1039, although here we have a different exit code (125 vs. 2).

[1]: https://travis-ci.org/projectatomic/libpod/jobs/399315452#L7006
[2]: https://github.com/projectatomic/libpod/blob/767b3ddc433fd57f461fa9866e3e4d47399a6519/test/e2e/ps_test.go#L233

comments.body: If podman can not find an image it returns 125.  I don't think this is an error, I think that Fedora for some reason did not have fedoraMinimal available for a while.
